title: Brachial Plexus Diagram
description: Diagram showing the brachial plexus formed from the ventral rami of C5 through T1 nerve roots, organized into roots, trunks (upper, middle, lower), anterior and posterior divisions, lateral/medial/posterior cords, and five terminal branches innervating the upper extremity.
clinical_notes: Erb's palsy (C5-C6 injury) causes the waiter's tip position — shoulder adducted, arm medially rotated, elbow extended. Klumpke's palsy (C8-T1) causes claw hand and may present with Horner's syndrome. Mnemonic for cords: 'My Lateral Cord Makes People Comfortable' (Musculocutaneous, Lateral pectoral, Median contributed, Posterior cord, Medial).
openstax_chapter: Chapter 13 – The Anatomy of the Nervous System (Unit 3: Regulation, Integration, and Control)
